channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal government launches financial audit of Office of the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages amid allegations and complaints
item.description: The federal government has ordered a financial audit into the Office of the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages after receiving anonymous complaints regarding its operations. Canadian Heritage confir...

item.title: Ottawa Police Investigate Disappearance of Man on Paddleboard in Ottawa River
item.description: Ottawa Police are actively searching for a man who was last observed entering the Ottawa River while on a paddleboard. Authorities have not released the man's identity, but the search effort involves ...

item.title: Infrastructure as a foundation for nation-building and inclusive development in South Africa
item.description: The article argues that infrastructure development in South Africa should be understood as a central pillar of nation-building rather than merely the delivery of physical assets. The author emphasises...

item.title: Italian authorities cancel Kanye West and Travis Scott concerts due to security and public order concerns
item.description: Italian authorities have officially cancelled planned concerts by Kanye West, now known as Ye, and Travis Scott in Reggio Emilia, northern Italy, citing significant security and public order concerns....

item.title: Israeli forces advance further into Lebanon amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has ordered Israeli troops to move further into Lebanon as part of continued military operations against Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group. The direct...

item.title: Bafana Bafana’s World Cup departure delayed due to visa issues
item.description: Bafana Bafana’s planned departure for the World Cup in Mexico has been delayed due to administrative challenges, primarily involving visa approvals. The South African Football Association (Safa) was s...

item.title: SAFA visa delays disrupt Bafana Bafana’s World Cup departure plans
item.description: Bafana Bafana’s preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup have been thrown into disarray after visa delays prevented several players and members of the technical team from travelling as scheduled to Me...

item.title: Benedix Ramos Takes on Challenging Role as a 12-Year-Old in Theater Production ‘Yemaya’
item.description: Actor Benedix Ramos has opened up about his latest theatre role in the production ‘Yemaya’, where he takes on the challenge of portraying a 12-year-old character. In an exclusive interview, Ramos shar...

item.title: Ukraine conducts strikes on Russian energy infrastructure while denying nuclear plant attack
item.description: Ukraine has intensified its drone strikes on Russian oil and gas facilities in recent months, claiming that these sites directly support Moscow's ongoing invasion, which has now lasted over four years...

item.title: Soweto Uprising legacy continues to inspire youth activism 50 years on
item.description: The article reflects on the enduring legacy of the June 16, 1976 Soweto uprising, fifty years later, and how it continues to shape youth activism in South Africa. The uprising, which began in response...

item.title: Atiku Abubakar and Hayatu-Deen Agree to Strengthen ADC Coalition Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Former Vice President of Nigeria and 2027 presidential hopeful on the platform of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has shared details of a recent closed-door meeting with fellow ...

item.title: Punjab authorises deployment of 6,000 police personnel to Gilgit-Baltistan for election security
item.description: Punjab authorities have approved the deployment of 6,000 police personnel to Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) to assist in security arrangements ahead of the upcoming elections scheduled for June 7. According to...

item.title: Swiatek bows out of French Open as Zverev and Ruud target quarterfinal spots
item.description: According to an Agence France-Presse report published on May 31, 2026, the French Open at Roland Garros continues to deliver major developments as top tennis names compete for a place in the later sta...

item.title: Frankie Valli pauses 2026 farewell tour dates to prioritise health
item.description: Frankie Valli, the legendary frontman of The Four Seasons, has announced the cancellation of his remaining 2026 tour dates, stating that he will take time away from performing to focus on his health. ...

item.title: PH government agencies enhance capabilities to detect and disrupt mobile phone scams
item.description: Government agencies in the Philippines have strengthened their capabilities to combat mobile-based scams through a coordinated technical training session focused on the use of IMSI Catcher Detectors. ...

item.title: Chadema and Registrar of Political Parties in renewed dispute over compliance and disciplinary warning
item.description: Chadema party has once again found itself in tension with the Office of the Registrar of Political Parties in Tanzania after receiving a fresh warning letter, even as it was presenting an assessment o...

item.title: Brooke Shields’ Childhood: Early Fame, Family Life, and Career Beginnings
item.description: Brooke Shields, born in Manhattan in 1965, had a childhood heavily shaped by early fame and family dynamics. Her parents, actress Teri Shields and businessman Francis Alexander 'Frank' Shields, divorc...

item.title: Elina Svitolina to face Marta Kostyuk as Ukraine reaches semi-finals
item.description: Elina Svitolina, one of Ukraine’s top tennis players, is preparing for her upcoming quarter-final match against fellow Ukrainian Marta Kostyuk. Svitolina expressed confidence and excitement ahead of t...

item.title: Guide to Accessing the May 31 Crossword and Its Solutions by Evan Birnholz
item.description: Evan Birnholz, a contributor to the Washington Post, has provided the solution to his May 31 crossword titled 'After-School Activities.' The crossword celebrates the post-graduate experience and is pa...

item.title: Western Cape High Court rules Cape Town’s value-linked service charges unlawful
item.description: On 30 April 2026, the Western Cape High Court ruled that Cape Town's fixed charges for citywide cleaning, water, and sanitation, which were based on property value, were unlawful and unconstitutional....
